etymology.description: ū¶•Ď(ū¶•Ď) (two pairs of hands) + ŚĽĺ(ŚĽĺ) (pair of hands) = both hands of two people working together -> final form ŤąĀ
etymology.interpretation: The character ŤąĀ represents the concept of cooperation, with the depiction of two sets of hands working together symbolizing joint effort. The original component ū¶•Ď indicates a pair of hands, reinforcing the idea of collaboration.
